title: lacinaria spicata f. resinosa
intro: Lacinaria spicata f. resinosa, also known as Resinous Sea Holly, is a plant species within the aster family, characterized by its prickly, resinous stems and purple flowers. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Lacinaria spicata f. resinosa include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and providing relief from skin irritations. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties.
